title: Presigned Downloads, CloudFront Signatures, Cookies, and Revocation · Language Lens: Cloudfront Url Cookie Choice
summary: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cloudfront url cookie choice increment in time-bounded signed-delivery decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
Presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Cloudfront Url Cookie Choice · Theory Cloudfront Url Cookie Choice
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3586 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound signed access by time, resource, key group, and explicit SHA-256 review; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
